title: Verify Tote Rack Centering Features And Tote Positioning
summary: Reference check to confirm the tote rack features described in training are present and that the tote position on the cylinder matches the documented centering and secure-positioning design intent.
when_to_use: Use when visually verifying that a tote rack matches the training description for tote placement, centering features, side fins, and beveled positioning design.
not_for: Do not use for repair, adjustment, or corrective maintenance actions.
Do not use to infer undocumented corrective actions if the rack does not match the training description.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the visual reference check and compare observed tote rack features to the training description.

safety_notes: This source supports a visual reference check only.
Do not infer repair or adjustment actions because the source describes component features and design intent only.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the tote rack
Training slide or source description of tote rack features

access_required: Visual access to the tote rack
system_preconditions: The tote rack is visible enough to inspect its tote position and physical centering features.

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing tote racking design features, tote position on the cylinder, centering feet, and side fins.

steps.title: Locate the tote rack and tote position
steps.purpose: Identify the rack structure referenced by the training and confirm where the tote sits.
steps.instruction: Locate the tote rack and identify the position where the tote sits on the cylinder. Compare the observed rack to the training image or description.
steps.expected_result: The tote rack is identified and the tote position on the cylinder is recognizable.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed rack matches the training description that the tote rack is where the tote sits on the cylinder.
steps.failure_condition: The rack or tote position cannot be matched to the training description.

actions.label: Identify tote rack location
actions.exact_action: Locate the tote rack and identify the position where the tote sits on the cylinder.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall tote rack structure and the area identified in training as where the tote sits on the cylinder.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed tote rack does not match the documented training description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The segment introduces tote racks and explains that this is where the tote sits on the cylinder.

steps.title: Verify centering feet are present
steps.purpose: Confirm the rack includes the documented features that align a dropped tote to center.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the rack includes the small feet described in the source and verify that these features align the dropped tote toward the center position.
steps.expected_result: Small feet are visible on the rack and match the training description of tote-centering features.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack includes the little feet described in training and they appear consistent with aligning the tote to the center.
steps.failure_condition: The little feet are not present, are not identifiable, or do not match the training description.

actions.label: Inspect centering feet
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the rack includes the small feet described in the source and verify that these features align the dropped tote toward the center position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The little feet or alignment points on the tote rack that the training says align the tote to the center.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed tote rack does not match the documented training description of feet or positioning features.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We have little feet so that when the tote drops, it is aligned to the center. It's pretty tight.

steps.title: Check for tight tote fit
steps.purpose: Confirm the observed tote fit matches the training description of a tight rack fit.
steps.instruction: Check whether the tote fit appears tight in the rack as described in the training segment.
steps.expected_result: The tote fit appears consistent with the training description of a tight fit.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote appears to sit tightly in the rack position.
steps.failure_condition: The tote fit does not appear tight or cannot be visually confirmed against the training description.

actions.label: Inspect tote fit
actions.exact_action: Check whether the tote fit appears tight in the rack as described in the training segment.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: How closely the tote position fits within the rack structure shown in the training frame.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed tote rack does not match the documented training description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We have little feet so that when the tote drops, it is aligned to the center. It's pretty tight.

steps.title: Verify side fins for package centering
steps.purpose: Confirm the rack includes the documented side fins intended to move off-center packages toward the middle.
steps.instruction: Observe the sides of the tote rack for fins and verify that the fins are positioned to help move packages toward the center if they hit off-center.
steps.expected_result: Side fins are visible and match the training description of package-centering features.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack includes side fins positioned in a way that is consistent with guiding off-center packages toward the center.
steps.failure_condition: The side fins are not present, are not identifiable, or do not match the training description.

actions.label: Inspect side fins
actions.exact_action: Observe the sides of the tote rack for fins and verify that the fins are positioned to help move packages toward the center if they hit off-center.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The fins on the side of the tote rack referenced in training as helping move packages toward the center.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed tote rack does not match the documented training description of side fins.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We also have these fins on the side that help move packages towards the center if they kind of hit.

steps.title: Compare observed rack to beveled design intent
steps.purpose: Confirm the observed rack is consistent with the documented design intent for secure tote positioning during parcel impact.
steps.instruction: Compare what is present on the rack to the documented design intent that the tote racking uses a beveled design to securely position the tote for parcel impact.
steps.expected_result: The observed rack features are consistent with the training description of a beveled design intended to securely position the tote.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack appearance and features align with the documented design intent for secure tote positioning and parcel impact handling.
steps.failure_condition: The observed rack does not appear consistent with the documented beveled design intent or secure-positioning description.

actions.label: Compare to design intent
actions.exact_action: Compare what is present on the rack to the documented design intent that the tote racking uses a beveled design to securely position the tote for parcel impact.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overall rack geometry and slide text indicating beveled design to securely position the tote for parcel impact.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed tote rack does not match the documented training description of feet, side fins, or positioning features.
Stop if corrective or repair action would be required, because this source does not provide those actions.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text references tote racking with a beveled design to securely position tote for parcel impact; transcript describes centering features.

success_criteria: The tote rack can be identified as the structure where the tote sits on the cylinder.
The small feet described in training are present or visually consistent with the documented centering function.
The tote fit appears tight as described in the training segment.
The side fins are present and visually consistent with guiding off-center packages toward the center.
The observed rack is consistent with the documented beveled design intent to securely position the tote for parcel impact.
healthy_conditions: Observed tote rack features match the training description.
Tote position appears centered and securely positioned within the rack.
Centering feet and side fins are identifiable and consistent with the documented design intent.
failure_conditions: Observed tote rack does not match the documented training description of feet, side fins, or positioning features.
Tote position on the rack cannot be matched to the training description.
The source does not provide corrective actions for mismatched or missing features.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed tote rack does not match the documented training description of feet, side fins, or positioning features.
Escalate if the tote rack cannot be visually confirmed against the training reference.
Do not infer repair or adjustment actions because the source describes component features and design intent only.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference image for tote rack structure, tote position on cylinder, centering feet, side fins, and beveled design intent.

missing_details: No formal corrective action is provided by the source if features are missing or do not match.
No time estimate is provided by the source.
No explicit production-stop or LOTO requirement is stated in the source.
No role boundary beyond operator-level visual verification is stated in the source.
